text.title: AWS Well-Architected Framework Checklists (2025-2026) Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Sustainability + Performance pillars left empty 'because nobody cares'; cost pillar gets 80% of the items.
antipattern.root-cause: Reviewer cherry-picks comfortable pillars; nobody enforces all-6-pillars-evaluated.
antipattern.fix: Block PR until every pillar has ≥1 item. Use the canonical item bank (`templates/wa-checklist.md`) as a floor.

antipattern.symptom: Checklist full of 'consider X', 'review Y'; review takes 4h, conclusions vary by reviewer.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wrote items as conversation starters, not gates.
antipattern.fix: Rewrite every item with a binary verb (`enabled` / `disabled` / `present` / `absent` / `>=` / `<=`). Map each to a CLI check.

antipattern.symptom: Items pass on review day; quarterly re-check shows different result with no audit trail of what changed.
antipattern.root-cause: Items rely on UI inspection or human memory.
antipattern.fix: Replace every item with a CLI/Config check. Wire into a nightly Lambda for drift detection.

antipattern.symptom: All 47 failed items shown as equal; team patches the easy ones; HIGH items linger.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped severity column; remediation triage delegated to gut feel.
antipattern.fix: Add severity (HIGH / MED / LOW) to every failed item based on impact + compliance scope. HIGH blocks launch.
